playbooks.title: Source discovery playbook
playbooks.summary: Find official board code, examples, headers, docs, datasheets, and framework references before precise implementation.
steps: Classify board, framework, peripheral, and evidence target.
Read official board repository examples and headers before lower-authority notes.
Use documentation repo and wiki as navigation, not as a replacement for code/header facts.
Run source query or source completeness when pins, buses, drivers, or demos are missing.
Return unknown_with_sources or needs_source_ingestion when exact facts are absent.
anti_claims: Source links cannot prove the user's firmware builds or runs.
Do not claim pins, buses, rails, or demos without source-backed facts.
resource_hints: Use project references for public extra sources with explanations.
Keep private logs, hosts, ports, and credentials in ignored local evidence.
benchmark_prompts: T-Display-S3 PlatformIO Arduino add LVGL sensor page
T-Watch Ultra add display driver BSP status action smoke

playbooks.title: Build, flash, and serial evidence playbook
playbooks.summary: Run a bounded build/upload/monitor loop with explicit permissions, bounded logs, and failure classification.
steps: Plan commands first and keep start dry-run unless explicit build/flash/serial permissions exist.
Build before upload and preserve the exact framework target used.
Open serial only after permission and capture bounded output.
Classify failures by build, upload, port, reset, no-log, or runtime panic.
Store large/raw logs in local evidence, not public context.
anti_claims: A generated command cannot prove flash success until the command runs.
Do not publish private serial ports, raw logs, or host paths.
resource_hints: serial-mcp-server can be recorded as a public serial-debug reference.
Use project-local preferences when the user prefers binflow or another transfer/debug tool.
benchmark_prompts: T-Watch Ultra Arduino build upload serial monitor debug
T-Display-S3 PlatformIO upload boot log fails

playbooks.title: LVGL display and touch debug playbook
playbooks.summary: Diagnose LVGL blank screen, flush, tick, draw buffer, touch, rotation, heap, simulator, and page-data loops without claiming rendered pixels.
steps: Confirm board display and touch facts from official examples before changing LVGL code.
Check tick, flush, invalidation, draw buffer, color order, and rotation in order.
For touch, separate controller probe from LVGL indev callback behavior.
Use simulator or page-data where available before claiming UI behavior.
Escalate to hardware or simulator evidence for rendered-pixel claims.
anti_claims: Source and route context cannot prove pixels rendered.
Do not claim touch works without callback or device evidence.
resource_hints: Read LVGL official docs first for core API behavior.
benchmark_prompts: T-Watch Ultra LVGL blank screen touch debug
T-Display-S3 PlatformIO Arduino add LVGL sensor page

playbooks.title: OTA manifest and rollback debug playbook
playbooks.summary: Diagnose OTA partition tables, manifests, digests, versions, transport, reboot, rollback, and project-local runners with evidence classification.
required_board_facts: project OTA runner or enough source references to create one
steps: Read framework OTA docs and project partition table before changing OTA code.
Validate manifest fields, digest, version, and binary target.
Resolve the project OTA runner from manifests, scripts, references, or ignored local state; ask only for private target details that cannot be inferred.
Observe reboot and rollback state through serial or project status endpoints.
Classify failures by manifest, transport, digest, partition, reboot, or rollback.
anti_claims: Treat source links and manifests as planning evidence until build, transport, or status evidence exists.
Keep Wi-Fi credentials, LAN hosts, OTA URLs, and private runner argv in local evidence.
resource_hints: Store resolved private OTA runner argv in .lilygo-skills/local.json.
Pair with build/flash/serial playbook for reboot observation.
benchmark_prompts: T-Watch Ultra ESP-IDF OTA rollback manifest debug
LilyGO ESP32-S3 OTA partition update fails

playbooks.title: BSP peripheral driver playbook
playbooks.summary: Wrap board peripherals behind source-backed BSP capability, status, action, smoke, and evidence contracts.
steps: Query board facts and preserve unknown_with_sources when exact IO is absent.
Read official example, chip datasheet, and framework driver before writing wrapper code.
Expose capability and status before action commands.
Make smoke checks bounded and non-destructive by default.
Record evidence level for each probe or action.
anti_claims: Driver patterns cannot create missing pin, bus, or power facts.
Do not claim a peripheral works without status/action/smoke evidence.
resource_hints: Use source completeness before guessing expander channels or rails.
benchmark_prompts: T-Watch Ultra add display driver BSP status action smoke
LilyGO ESP32-S3 add peripheral driver capability status

playbooks.title: Radio and GNSS source navigation playbook
playbooks.summary: Navigate LoRa, radio, GNSS, RadioLib, Meshtastic-style references, regional settings, antennas, and RF/GNSS evidence boundaries.
diagnostic_axes: RF packet or GNSS fix evidence
steps: Read official LilyGO board examples before choosing RadioLib or Meshtastic references.
Confirm chip and pin facts; do not merge LoRa and GNSS into one generic fact.
Check regional frequency and antenna requirements before radio tests.
For GNSS, separate UART/NMEA visibility from actual fix acquisition.
Treat Meshtastic references as application guidance only when requested.
evidence_targets: RF packet log or GNSS fix report
anti_claims: Source refs cannot prove RF links or GNSS fixes.
Do not claim legal region/frequency correctness without user/project facts.
resource_hints: RadioLib is a library reference, not a board fact source.
Meshtastic is application guidance only when the user asks for it.
benchmark_prompts: T-Beam Arduino LoRa GNSS RadioLib debug
LilyGO Meshtastic LoRa GPS source navigation

playbooks.title: Setup and toolchain readiness playbook
playbooks.summary: Plan blank-machine setup for Rust, Node, Arduino CLI, PlatformIO, ESP-IDF, esp-rs, serial, and flash tools without silently installing anything.
steps: Check prerequisites and report missing tools before install.
Ask or use project context for framework selection when absent.
Use setup plan dry-run to list commands and private inputs.
Install only after user authorization and keep logs bounded.
Record preferences for framework order or debug tools when the user requests them.
anti_claims: Do not silently install Rust, Node, firmware toolchains, or drivers.
A setup plan cannot prove a firmware build until the build command runs.
resource_hints: Respect project preferences for framework order and debug tools.
Use public project references for extra setup docs; keep private machine facts local.
benchmark_prompts: LilyGO T-Display-S3 blank machine setup PlatformIO Arduino
LilyGO ESP32-S3 install ESP-IDF Rust serial tools
